title: Make every task idempotent per execution date
description: Parameterize tasks by the run's logical date and write so that rerunning the same date overwrites rather than duplicates.
body: Use when a task writes data and could be retried or backfilled. Do not append unconditionally — a retry or backfill then duplicates rows and corrupts downstream aggregates. Actions: parameterize the task by its execution/logical date, delete-and-replace or merge the target partition for that date, and verify a second run for the same date yields identical output. Validation signals include a rerun producing the same partition with no duplication and a backfill matching the original scheduled output. Risk boundary: a non-idempotent task makes retries and backfills unsafe; if a task cannot overwrite its own date partition cleanly, treat it as unsafe to retry and escalate to redesign the write before enabling automatic retries.
